Import TensorFlow, TensorFlow Datasets, Keras, NumPy, and Matplotlib modules required for data handling, model building, and visualization
Load the COCO 2017 dataset splits 'train' and 'validation' using tfds.load with data_dir='data' to ensure dataset is cached locally
Define batch size as 2 for training and 1 for validation to match example setup

Implement the preprocess_data function that resizes images with shortest side jitter in range [640, 1024], caps longest side at 1333, applies random horizontal flipping, and converts bounding boxes from [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] to [x, y, width, height]
Map preprocess_data over both train and validation datasets with num_parallel_calls=tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Use padded_batch on train dataset with batch_size=2, padding_values=(0.0, 1e-8, -1), and drop_remainder=True to create fixed-shape batches with padding for images, bounding boxes, and class ids
Chain a call to label_encoder.encode_batch on the train dataset batches to convert raw labels to training targets; similarly apply on validation dataset with batch_size=1 padded batches
Apply tf.data.experimental.ignore_errors() on both train and validation datasets to silently skip erroneous samples during iteration
Prefetch datasets with tf.data.AUTOTUNE to improve pipeline throughput
Build the RetinaNet model with ResNet50 backbone and custom feature pyramid, classification and box regression heads as defined in the example
Compile the model with RetinaNetLoss as loss function and SGD optimizer with learning rate schedule matching the example (PiecewiseConstantDecay with specific boundaries and values)
Set epochs=3 to allow multiple epochs for training to trigger the error after first epoch

Train the model using model.fit with train_dataset.take(100) for training and val_dataset.take(50) for validation, batch_size=2 for training and 1 for validation, callbacks including ModelCheckpoint, and verbose=1
Monitor training logs for successful completion of first epoch and observe the loss values (e.g., total loss around 21.5, box_loss ~2.6, class_loss ~19) before error occurs

At the end of the first epoch, verify that an UnknownError occurs originating from keras_cv.metrics.object_detection.box_coco_metrics.py in the call to tf.concat, with message indicating mismatched dimension 1 in shapes [4, 2, 4] vs [4, 1, 4]
Confirm the stack trace shows the error triggered during the evaluation callback's on_epoch_end method when invoking self.metrics.result(force=True)
Assert that the error is reproducible on a Colab Pro environment with TensorFlow 2.x, KerasCV installed, and COCO 2017 dataset loaded as specified
Verify that the bug occurs specifically after completing the first epoch and during metric calculation on validation dataset
Document the exact error message and stack trace indicating the dimension mismatch in tf.concat operation
